welcome.title: Welcome to MultiGraph Lab
welcome.intro: A browser app to build and explore relationship graphs — entities as nodes, typed links — with AI-assisted analysis and synthesis at its core.
welcome.whatBody: Each node is an entity (person, organization, concept, etc.). Edges are relationships between them. The graph shows structure, dependencies and connections at a glance.
welcome.purposeBody: Model networks, org charts, knowledge maps, or any topic you want to map. Everything runs in your browser: no account and no server required. Data stays on your device.
welcome.aiTitle: AI: the core of the app
welcome.aiIntro: The AI tab guides you through an external assistant (ChatGPT, Claude, etc.): copy the built-in prompts, paste JSON responses, then view and refine the graph.
welcome.aiStep1: Create a graph from a document — analyze text (article, notes, report) to extract entities and relationships.
welcome.aiStep2: Import the graph — paste the AI's JSON response to load the graph into the app.
welcome.aiStep3: Enrich the graph — send the current graph to the AI to add or refine entities and links.
welcome.aiStep4: Text synthesis — produce a written summary and commentary of the graph.
welcome.step1: Create tab → Entity: add nodes (name, type, optional description).
welcome.step2: Create tab → Link: connect two entities with a relationship type (names or click nodes on the graph).
welcome.step3: Browse: search, filter by type, and select entities; the graph can focus on your selection.
welcome.step4: Detail: view or edit the selected entity or relationship.
welcome.step5: Graph: drag to pan, scroll to zoom, click to select. Toolbar: link mode, reorganize layout, undo/redo.
welcome.offlineNote: Your graph is saved locally in this browser. You can install the app (PWA) for quick access from your home screen.

confirm.resetMessage: All entities, links and relationship types will be deleted. This cannot be undone.

ai.hint: Workflow: 1) create a graph from a document, 2) improve it, 3) import the AI JSON response, 4) generate a textual synthesis.
